title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
Job Description Library Overview

Job descriptions are organized by function in directories in the content-sites/handbook repository .

View job descriptions in the Job Description Library .

For any questions related to job descriptions, please reach out to your Manager.

Things to Keep in Mind

Before you begin creating a new job description you should check the directory to see if there is an existing job description or a role close to the one you are working to create. Job families should encompass a team, a department, or perhaps a division. Sometimes you should just add a level, a specialty or a segment and not create a new job description.

Sometimes you should just add a level, a specialty or a segment and not create a new job description.

We don’t include location requirements for most roles (EMEA, Americas, APAC) in the job description because these can change over time and job descriptions are constant.

We don’t include expertises , since these are free form.

We don’t include a specific number of years of experience unless they are directly relevant to the ability to perform in the role at the level required. If the years of experience are not directly relevant to the role, then we instead say that a person in a specified role should be able to show ‘relevant, progressive experience’ and then define what constitutes relevant, progressive experience in that particular context. For grade 4 & 5 roles, we avoid listing any kind of prior experience requirements to encourage people with unique career or educational journeys to see themselves in a particular role.

text:

For grade 4 & 5 roles, we avoid listing any kind of prior experience requirements to encourage people with unique career or educational journeys to see themselves in a particular role.

We don’t allow them to read like a vacancy. The job description could be used for hiring however they are more often serving as the requirements that team members and managers alike use in conversations around career development and performance management. The verbiage you don’t want to use includes phrases like “you will”, or “exciting opportunity” or “we’re hiring for”.

We are continually iterating on job descriptions. Do not search other job descriptions for examples of what good looks like. The templates below and this page are the SSOT for guidance.

Job Descriptions should be consistent with GitLab level expectations. Please refer to the Job Frameworks for guidance on expectations related to Scope and Influence, Complexity and Problem Solving, and Leadership and Communication requirements by level for both Individual Contributors and People Managers.

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
or guidance.

Job Descriptions should be consistent with GitLab level expectations. Please refer to the Job Frameworks for guidance on expectations related to Scope and Influence, Complexity and Problem Solving, and Leadership and Communication requirements by level for both Individual Contributors and People Managers.

Team Lead vs people Manager vs function Manager: you should be aware of how GitLab defines these terms. Depending on the needs of the organization, a Team Lead may or may not directly manage people. There are 2 instances where a Team Lead role can be used: Team Lead as an Individual Contributor - in this context, the Team Lead would be responsible for a specific project/s or area of expertise (a Subject Matter Expert), and may partner with others to accomplish the work. The Team Lead can be leveraged as a player/coach, where they have their own individual tasks to complete, but they also mentor, train and coach others on the team. A team lead may also handle escalated or complex cases or projects. Team Lead as a People Manager - in this context, the Team Lead would directly manage a team of people as a first level or entry-level people manager, which means they are directly responsible for hiring, promoting, performance management, or termination of team members. Team Leads can report into Managers, even though they may share the same compensation benchmark. Manager job descriptions are those responsible for directly managing other GitLab team-members. They hire, promote, and terminate team members, and performance management is one of their key functions. Nomenclature to represent someone is a manager of people can be clarified with “Manager, Benchmark” (e.g., Manager, Corporate Events). The same holds true for director (e.g. Director, Corporate Events).

The same holds true for director (e.g. Director, Corporate Events).

Nomenclature to represent when someone manages a job function, and does not have people management responsibilities, can be clarified with “Benchmark Manager” (e.g., Marketing Manager)

Team Leads can report into Managers, even though they may share the same compensation benchmark.

Anyone can create or edit a job description, but generally it is the responsibility of the person who will manage the position or job description to kick off the approval process. They may enlist help from others as necessary. After a merge request is created for an existing or new job description, the merge request must follow the approval flows below :

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
ription, but generally it is the responsibility of the person who will manage the position or job description to kick off the approval process. They may enlist help from others as necessary. After a merge request is created for an existing or new job description, the merge request must follow the approval flows below :

Your manager : Your direct manager is responsible for clarifying the scope of responsibilities and level of roles, ensuring clear differentiation between levels, and checking responsibilities and requirements and ensuring the job description follows the template logic and has all of the required areas . Mangers should validate business need for the role with their direct manager. Any review after this should be quick so the manager is the gatekeeper.

Your manager : Your direct manager is responsible for clarifying the scope of responsibilities and level of roles, ensuring clear differentiation between levels, and checking responsibilities and requirements and ensuring the job description follows the template logic and has all of the required areas . Mangers should validate business need for the role with their direct manager. Any review after this should be quick so the manager is the gatekeeper.

Talent Management : Talent Management will review for job level expectations consistency with other similarly leveled roles across the organization, alignment to our GitLab Job Frameworks , and overall structure, then notify your People Business Partner (PBP).

Your Department Head and People Business Partner : Your Department Head (typically at the VP level) is responsible for confirming there is a business need for the role and will review department/division structure and levels in partnership with the aligned People Business Partner. If the role is being created due to headcount planning, please ensure your Talent Acquisition partner has visibility for recruiting purposes.

Templates for New Job Description

There are two templates on this page. The first template is appropriate when the job description has 1 or 2 job titles and more levels (i.e. junior, intermediate, senior, manager, director) or specialties; for example . There can be many levels for each of the titles. The second template is appropriate when there are many different job titles within the same family; for example .

Job Description Template 1

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
iption has 1 or 2 job titles and more levels (i.e. junior, intermediate, senior, manager, director) or specialties; for example . There can be many levels for each of the titles. The second template is appropriate when there are many different job titles within the same family; for example .

Job Description Template 1

After pasting this template search for {add to quickly navigate to areas of input.

Template 1 Additional Details

List levels in ascending order. Start w/ junior/associate and end with the highest level.

List any overarching responsibilities or requirements that are applicable for the entire job description before listing the levels.

Do not repeat the junior requirement list for more senior levels: You do not want to relist an entire requirement set from a more junior level that also pertains to a more senior one. You can choose to keep the section title as {Level} Requirements. You can then add a first bullet that says, “Extends the {name of lower job level} responsibilities” or “Extends the {name of lower job level} requirements.”

You do not want to relist an entire requirement set from a more junior level that also pertains to a more senior one. You can choose to keep the section title as {Level} Requirements. You can then add a first bullet that says, “Extends the {name of lower job level} responsibilities” or “Extends the {name of lower job level} requirements.”

Specialities may be relevant for some but not all levels. Clarify which specialties each level pertains to. If there are no specialties remove the section.

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
ments. You can then add a first bullet that says, “Extends the {name of lower job level} responsibilities” or “Extends the {name of lower job level} requirements.”

Specialities may be relevant for some but not all levels. Clarify which specialties each level pertains to. If there are no specialties remove the section.

Segment : A Segment is something you will see with Sales based Job Descriptions (Example, Enterprise, Mid-market, SMB, Public Sector). If there is no segment for the job description remove the section.

If performance indicators are the same for all the levels on the job description do not list them under the levels section, rather list them at the end of the entire job description. Remove either the performance indicator section in the level section or at the end of the template.

Career Ladder: The next job description. All levels should be on one job description, except when the next job description manages over multiple job descriptions. For example . Some teams added a Career Path: * Moving to and moving from - which describes possible current and future roles which move to the job description and which the job description might move to. Here is an example .

The Compensation and About GitLab sections will auto-populate because of the job description formatting.

Job Description Template 2

The second template is appropriate when there are three or more job titles within the same family. For example the team is made up of many different positions, that all report into the same person or are all working to accomplish the same goals.

Creating a New Job Description from Scratch

Here is a brief walkthrough of this process. If you use these slides please remember that the HANDBOOK is the most up to date and the slides and/or videos may be dated.

Go to the content-sites/handbook project on gitlab.com

Select the content directory

Go to the job-description-library directory

Select the department where you will be making the new job description (i.e. Marketing, Sales, etc)

Once you are in the correct department directory, select the +

From the drop down list, select New file

Insert the title in the format of job-title.md for example marketing-campaign-manager.md

Copy the Template for New Job Description and insert it into the body of the file

Fill in the sections of the template by replacing everything in {curly brackets} search {add

Use markdown formatting

Delete any unnecessary sections - for example, there may be no Levels or Specialties at this time, so those sections can be deleted

Update the Commit message with a description of what you are doing

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
Fill in the sections of the template by replacing everything in {curly brackets} search {add

Use markdown formatting

Delete any unnecessary sections - for example, there may be no Levels or Specialties at this time, so those sections can be deleted

Update the Commit message with a description of what you are doing

Update Target Branch to an abbreviation of what you did. Note: include dashes between words instead of spaces.

Make sure “Start a new merge request with these changes” box is checked.

Click the Commit changes button at the bottom

Check: Delete source branch when merge request is accepted.

Click the Submit merge request button at the bottom

Creating an Overview page

In cases where a job description is quite large you may decide to create an overview page of the job description. This helps when we are actively sourcing for external candidates on a role.

Keep in mind that the overview page needs to be updated when you add a new level or a new role to a job description.

The opening statement about the job description should be copied from the job description page to the overview page. Because not all job descriptions have overview pages you should not remove it from one or the other.

The overview page should follow the typical naming convention by adding a sub folder overview

For every overview page we created, the page has the URL /job-description-library/department/title/overview/ and should be
created starting from the template. There’s only a few additions you should do with the template.

New Job Description Overview creation

New Job Description Overview creation

Create the folder in /job-description-library/[department]/[name-of-job-family]/overview , being sure to use only lower case in naming your directory if it doesn’t already exist, and add it to the correct department subdirectory.

We have a CI job that checks job descriptions for hard requirements:

Requirements every role must have Ability to use GitLab as a bullet point.

every role must have Ability to use GitLab as a bullet point.

Performance Indicators

Inclusive Language Check

Inclusive Language Check

We use an open source Ruby gem to perform a inclusive language check on all job descriptions.

Currently that library covers:

the usage of masculine vs feminine-coded language

the usage of gendered pronouns

fixed vs growth mindset

The following results lead to failure of the pipeline:

High usage of masculine-coded language: this means the job description is using more masculine-coded words than feminine-coded words.

High usage of masculine-coded language: this means the job description is using more masculine-coded words than feminine-coded words.

Usage of any misused words as this could excluded diverse groups.

Usage of any misused words as this could excluded diverse groups.

High usage of fixed-coded language: this means the job description is using more fixed-coded words than growth-coded.

High usage of fixed-coded language: this means the job description is using more fixed-coded words than growth-coded.

diverse groups.

Usage of any misused words as this could excluded diverse groups.

High usage of fixed-coded language: this means the job description is using more fixed-coded words than growth-coded.

High usage of fixed-coded language: this means the job description is using more fixed-coded words than growth-coded.

Usage of gendered pronouns: we want to be inclusive to all. If you’re using language that is marked as subtly masculine-coded, fixed-coded or using misused words, the pipeline will fail and you will need to fix it before following the approval flow. Please reference (and add to) this list for suggested words to use in place of masculine or fixed language. If the pipeline does fail, the first recommendation is to read the error. It will say what is wrong with the text. For example: 1. ["ATTENTION: In /builds/gitlab-com/www-gitlab-com/sites/uncategorized/source/job-description-library/marketing/reference-program-manager/index.html.md you're using masculine gender-coded language", "Masculine coded words used: analyst, analytical, decision, driven, leader"] 2. ["ATTENTION: In /builds/gitlab-com/www-gitlab-com/sites/uncategorized/source/job-description-library/marketing/production-designer/index.html.md you're using fixed-coded language", "Fixed coded words used: established"] In this case, there are two job descriptions that failed and each for a different reason. You can do two things now:
- fix the text to be more inclusive, commit your changes and the pipeline will run again
- if you disagree or you feel like the pipeline found a false positive, you can add the file to this list . The files in this list are ignored by the inclusiveness check. If you request your job description to be added to the skip level you will need to paste a screenshot of the inclusiveness check in the MR as this will be asked for prior to merging.

Why Job Descriptions Have Performance Indicators

All job descriptions should have performance indicators (PIs).

title: Job Description Library Overview
text:
he inclusiveness check. If you request your job description to be added to the skip level you will need to paste a screenshot of the inclusiveness check in the MR as this will be asked for prior to merging.

Why Job Descriptions Have Performance Indicators

All job descriptions should have performance indicators (PIs).

Performance indicators should tell you whether or not you’re doing your job well or correctly. For example, our Vice President, Investor Relations role has performance indicators that compare how we’re being described by analysts to how we describe ourselves.

Performance indicators are important because people want to know that they’re contributing.

Candidates want to know what success looks like in a role.

Team Members want to know how they’re being measured for success.

Company, functional, or department KPIs are too generic and, thus, not useful as job description performance indicators, only a sub-set of performance indicators are KPIs.

Time sensitive PIs, like OKRs or weekly goals, are also not useful because they are not long term success factors.

Performance indicators should be specific to the role and not dependent on anyone else’s performance.

Job Descriptions should have three to five PIs.

Performance Indicators should be linked to one or more performance indicator definitions for which this job description will be the DRI .
